name: develop-frontend
description: Next.js/Reactによるフロントエンド実装スキル（UI、API連携、状態管理、テスト）
capabilities: Implement Next.js App Router pages with proper directory structure
Create Presentational and Container components following project conventions
Integrate APIs using Orval-generated clients with proper error handling
Write unit tests with Vitest and Testing Library achieving 80% coverage
Apply shadcn/ui components and styling with Tailwind CSS
Run lint checks, tests, and builds to verify code quality
author: Crearize
securitySummary: Pure markdown documentation skill containing only frontend development guidance. No executable code, scripts, or dangerous capabilities. All static findings are false positives: cryptographic hash references in metadata were misidentified as weak crypto, shell command examples in documentation were flagged as backtick execution, and standard YAML frontmatter was flagged as obfuscation. This is a guidance-only skill that instructs AI agents on development workflows.
valueStatement: Frontend development requires following specific project conventions, testing standards, and build processes. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for implementing Next.js/React features with proper component architecture, API integration, and test coverage.
limitations: This is a guidance skill that assists developers; it does not execute code directly
Requires an existing Next.js/React project structure to follow
Assumes access to local development tools (pnpm, Node.js)

useCases.title: Build new features
useCases.description: Implement new pages and components following project conventions with proper testing

useCases.title: Create UI components
useCases.description: Develop reusable Presentational components with Storybook and testing

useCases.title: Implement test coverage
useCases.description: Write unit and integration tests for React components using Vitest

promptTemplates.title: New page request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new dashboard page at views/dashboard/ with user statistics display. Include a Container component that fetches data using the Orval API client and Presentational components for the UI. Write Vitest tests with 80% coverage.

promptTemplates.title: Component creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a reusable UserCard component using shadcn/ui Card. Create the component in components/UserCard/ with index.tsx, index.test.tsx, and index.stories.tsx. Apply tablet-first responsive design.

promptTemplates.title: Form implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a contact form using React Hook Form and Zod validation. Use shadcn/ui Form components. Include proper error handling and loading states. Write tests for validation and submission.

promptTemplates.title: API integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Integrate the user profile API. Create a useUserProfile custom hook that handles loading, error, and success states. Use the Orval-generated client. Write tests with MSW mock handlers.

bestPractices: Always check existing shadcn/ui components before creating new ones
Separate Presentational (UI) and Container (logic) components clearly
Run lint, tests, and build locally before reporting completion
antiPatterns: Using any type instead of unknown for type safety
Mixing business logic with UI rendering in same component
Skipping test coverage requirements for faster delivery

faq.question: What tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Requires pnpm, Node.js, and Next.js project.

faq.question: What testing frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Vitest for unit tests, Testing Library for behavior tests, MSW for API mocking.

faq.question: Can this skill create full-stack features?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on frontend. Backend API integration uses pre-generated Orval clients.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-only skill. All code runs locally in your development environment.

faq.question: Why is my build failing?
faq.answer: Check Biome lint errors, TypeScript strict mode issues, and ensure all imports are used. Run 'pnpm lint:check' locally.

faq.question: How does this compare to other frontend skills?
faq.answer: This skill enforces specific project conventions including component structure, testing requirements, and coding standards.
